betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Worthiness takes many forms, and the mysterious force governing Gallifrey does not discriminate.
One who achieves greatness through their own effort is not necessarily one who is worthy, but one who freely donates their time and talents for another's sake is indeed worthy, even when the outcome is an undesired one.
Those that fell for a just cause, they too are worthy.
I am reassured, and quite happy, to see Fractallites capable of bringing good to the Universe. The power of Gallifrey is definitely one that abides to a great ideal.
If you would have asked my past self, back on Earth, who God was, I would have gone on a long monologue about the aspects of divinity; a great love, the ability to give meaning to hard lives, mercy, power used for absolute good, respect for freedom, an inspiration to creativity.
After reaching Terra, I cannot deny it, nihilism and cynicism came closer to how I would have described divinity. Creatures not that much different from the gods of ancient Mythology. Petty, vicious creatures. Just like people.
And yet, as I Ascended, I entered the path of Mission, one that is not only larger than myself, but larger. Full stop. Larger, like the idea of God I had back on Earth.
And this mission is something to strive towards, an ideal, a beautiful perfection, or rather, an aspect of beautiful perfection, just like one of the aspects of divity I used to list out.
An aspect I see here in Gallifrey. A world not devoid of struggles and conflict, and yet a world that feels just. Struggles exists, but it is not in vain. 
If I am to be a Fractallite, I want to be like the Fractallite of Gallifrey. 
I wonder if I will ever meet them...
